text: We did an online purchase for pickup at this location for our daughters necklace. I was in the area and this store stated it was the last one available online so I made sure to purchase before my meeting was over so I could pick up afterwards at 5 pm. My gf Kim left her wallet at home but had the order number, the barcode to scan, and a non-government (nursing) ID with full name. When purchasing online it did not state that pickup required a government ID. The store manager was very rude and not accommodating. She refused to give her the purchased item. We have picked up at the Belden Village store which is more near our home but did not have the Mickey mouse necklace and I have used my nursing ID, order number, and barcode with success at that location. I explained that to the store clerk and she told me she "highly doubts that", so now we also lying. When we explained we'd like to cancel and repurchase since this is the last of this item, she stated very rudely "well you shouldn't trust the online inventory". Putting it back on the customer for the companies shortsightedness. She however had no problem cancelling it without a government ID and using the order number. I now will need to return with my ID to get this item she states.  When asked if she could cancel the item and we repurchase it, the manager stated that we cannot get the discount again and they cannot ship it. We are disappointed in the inconsistency of her store policy. We see that as a common theme for this store in the reviews so we will not be returning.

text: I cannot NOT recommend this store enough.  I travel for work and purchase Pandora charms during my travel.  I have never experienced this level of rudeness, disrespect and unprofessionalism during a shopping experience.  I arrived to what I thought was a somewhat busy store with no line; but soon realized there was a line outside on the other side of the entrance.  I did not approach an associate nor did I try to get anyone’s attention as my time was limited that day.  I simply wanted to browse for NC specific items and would then order online. I was approached my a sales associate and asked to leave the store.  I was shocked & embarrassed & very much angered but did not react in a negative way.  I simply asked why & was told that there was a line outside & that I needed to stand in it. NO SIGNAGE…..NO DIRECTION…..AND treated like a criminal.  Once I did respond that I was doing a quick glance, due to the realization of the line & time restraint; I was advised that I was not welcome in the store & needed to leave.  I DID NOT expect to be allowed to jump a line or take an associate away from someone in line.  Do better.
address: 4325 Glenwood Ave, Raleigh, NC 27612, United States
